title: Tomophyllum duriusculum
taxonomy: Tomophyllum duriusculum is a species of fern in the family Polypodiaceae. It is native to tropical Asia, specifically in countries such as Malaysia, the Philippines, and Indonesia. Tomophyllum duriusculum is a member of the genus Tomophyllum, which is a small genus of ferns within the family Polypodiaceae. The classification of this species is as follows: Kingdom: Plantae, Division: Pteridophyta, Class: Polypodiopsida, Order: Polypodiales, Family: Polypodiaceae, Genus: Tomophyllum, Species: T. duriusculum. Further research may be needed for a more comprehensive understanding of its classification.

intro: Tomophyllum duriusculum, also known as Duria, is a species of fern native to the tropical regions of Asia. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which may aid in the treatment of various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Tomophyllum duriusculum include flavonoids, glycosides, and sesquiterpenes, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
